titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the International Festival of Arts and Ideas Records
International Festival of Arts and Ideas Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Christy Tomecek

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday April 17, 2021 at 09:15

did.unittitle: International Festival of Arts and Ideas records
did.abstract: This collection consists of the records of the International Festival of Arts and Ideas, a New Haven, Connecticut festival, devoted to theater, dance, music, visual arts, other performance arts, and scholarly discussions. The records document the runnning and planning of the festival, and include minutes, agendas, correspondence, financial records, programs and other festival ephemera, and video recordings of festival events and training protocols. Administrative files and fundraising files comprise the majority of the collection, which document the broader financial and staffing needs for running the festival, as well as the nucleus committee that brought the festival into existence. Programming and planning files are also present, as well as film of performances. There are a small number of files documenting activities of the Board of Directors, but these files are not complete for any period of time of the festival's existence. Materials cover from 1988 to 2013, with the bulk from 1995 to 2006. In general, the earliest years of the festival are more extensively documented than later years.

corpname.part: International Festival of Arts and Ideas

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged in five series: I. Administrative files, 1988-2011. II. Board of Director files, 1990-2013. III. Fundraising files, 1995-2012. IV. Festival planning and programming files, 1995-2013. V. Performance recordings and materials, 1996-2000.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The International Festival of Arts and Ideas was conceived in 1995 by Anne Calabresi, Jean Handley and Roslyn "Roz" Meyer. Their goal was to create an annual celebration in New Haven, Connecticut that would bring together artists and thinkers from around the world, ranging from the very established to newer ones starting their careers. The festival was officially incorporated in 1996. Types of performances include theater, solo performance work, music, puppet work, slam poetry and photography exhibitions. Ideas related programs are equally as varied and has included such programs as comparing and contrasting literature in the United States and China, analysis of music about wars and soldiers, using the arts to help high-risk youths, and the state of the ecosystem of Long Island Sound. The city of New Haven is heavily utilized for the festival, with venues including Yale University, the Shubert Theater, the New Haven Green, shopping centers, and even street corners. There is also family programming, tours of different neighborhoods and institutions in the city, and master classes on a variety of topics. The festival celebrated its 20th year in 2015.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research. Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives website.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced for fifty (50) years from the date of creation of materials in the collection, or upon the date Donor ceases to exist, whichever occurs first, after which copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the International Festival of Arts and Ideas, 2015.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: International Festival of Arts and Ideas Records (MS 2021). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of the records of the International Festival of Arts and Ideas, a New Haven, Connecticut festival, devoted to theater, dance, music, visual arts, other performance arts, and scholarly discussions. The records document the runnning and planning of the festival, and include minutes, agendas, correspondence, financial records, programs and other festival ephemera, and video recordings of festival events and training protocols. Administrative files and fundraising files comprise the majority of the collection, which document the broader financial and staffing needs for running the festival, as well as the nucleus committee that brought the festival into existence. Programming and planning files are also present, as well as film of performances. There are a small number of files documenting activities of the Board of Directors, but these files are not complete for any period of time of the festival's existence. Materials cover from 1988 to 2013, with the bulk from 1995 to 2006. In general, the earliest years of the festival are more extensively documented than later years.

corpname.part: International Festival of Arts and Ideas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I consists of administrative files that document the broader staffing and financial needs of the festival, as well as its structural and strategic planning. Also included are press clippings of the festival and government citations honoring the festival. There are day-to-day financial records represented in these files but for information about its funding sources and funding planning, please see Series III, Fundraising files. Finally, the nucleus committee files that document the founding, naming, and incorporation of the festival are located in this series. Series I is organized alphabetically by topic, then title of folder.

did.unittitle: Festival stationery, form letters, and guides
Greater New Haven Arts and Entertainment Facilities Study
Greater New Haven Arts and Entertainment Facilities Study
Market feasibility analysis, International Festival of Arts and Ideas

title.part: Performances on the Green, Final Report

did.unittitle: Officers notes on individual staff members
Center for American Music (CAM) and Jazz and American Music Museum (JAMM)
1997 operating plan, management objectives towards operating plan
2003 strategic plan, drafts and final copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II comprises materials produced by and for the Board of Directors of the festival. Much of this material consists of minutes, agendas, and related correspondence for different Board meetings, as well as their Executive Committee and their Nominating and Governance Committee (board members are the only ones who can serve on those committees). The Executive Committee determines festival goals while the Nominating and Governance Committee suggests new board candidates to fill vacancies. For activities of the Development Committee, see Series III, Fundraising files. For the activities of the Financial Committee, see Series I, Administrative files. For the activities of the Planning Committee, see Series IV, Planning and programming files. There are also guiding documents such as handbooks, regulations, and orientation materials. Series II is arranged by document type.

did.unittitle: Background research on forming a board of directors
Board contact list and meeting dates
Example evaluations of University of New Haven board
"Expanding the Minority Pipeline: A Guide for Board Members"
Mini-retreat on resource development and board engagement
Committee paperwork to be sorted and filed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains records related to the different methods of fundraising done for the festival. This includes individual and corporate donors, funding designated by government budgets and government programs, grants earned by the festival, and corporate sponsorship opportunities, including in-kind donations. Materials include correspondence, data about sources of funding, applications for funding, and guides for fundraising. The records of the Development Committee are also present in this series. Series III is organized first by topic, than alphabetically.

did.unittitle: Flier for American Lung Association of Connecticut benefit
Planned Giving Group of Connecticut all day conference binder
Qunnipiack Club candidate list for board of governors
Sikorsky Aircraft and United Technology Corporation
List of foundation sources for funding
Article related to fiscal year 2008 state budget
Government and Community Affairs Task Force
Report on meeting with Toni Harp
Testimony and supporting documents for Appropriations Committee public hearing
Community Foundation of New Haven Renewal Grant
Connecticut Commission on the Arts promotional logo
Greater New Haven Arts Stabilization project program design
Greater New Haven Arts Stabilization project terms
Strategic plan drafting and related meetings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV contains materials related to the planning, logistics, and marketing of festival events and performances. Materials include correspondence, press releases, promotional ephemera and programs, training videos, prospective artist information, and tour materials. Series IV is arranged by festival year, then alphabetically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V consists of video recordings of performances and seminars that took place in the early years of the festival. Almost all of them are from the 1997 and 1998 festivals. Series V is arranged alphabetically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.
